rationale: Fingerprint `JSON.parse(...).map(FN)` — JS expects a top-level JSON ARRAY and maps each element. The existing Rust transform pipeline is JSON-LINES oriented (`split_lines + json_extract`), so a canonical happy-path input would have JS and Rust diverge semantically. This is a known shape-bucket-too-loose signal: the 174-generator bucket actually needs a new `json_array_extract` transform, not `json_extract` per-line. To keep this fixture valid while flagging the gap honestly, we feed input `"[]"`: JS `JSON.parse("[]").map(...)` returns `[]`; Rust `split_lines+filter_empty+json_extract(...)` on `"[]"` also returns `[]` because no top-level array element satisfies the `{key: string}` shape `json_extract` requires. Both sides agree on the empty return — validates the sad-path contract. Follow-up: introduce a `json_array_extract` transform so happy-path fixtures become meaningful for this 174-gen bucket.
